knowledge_base.question: What is CKPCET?
knowledge_base.answer: C. K. Pithawala College of Engineering and Technology (CKPCET) is a self-financed engineering institute that started functioning in December 1998. It is affiliated to Gujarat Technological University (GTU), Ahmedabad, and managed by the Navyug Vidyabhavan Trust — a Public Education Trust registered under Bombay Public Trust Act 1950 on 21 February 1965. The institute is approved by AICTE and offers undergraduate (B.E.) and postgraduate (M.E.) programs in multiple engineering disciplines. CKPCET is known for academic excellence, industry exposure, and practical learning in a green, peaceful campus environment in Surat, Gujarat.

knowledge_base.question: Where is CKPCET located? What is the address?
knowledge_base.answer:
CKPCET is located in Surat, Gujarat.

📍 Full Address:
C. K. Pithawala College of Engineering and Technology,
Opposite Surat Airport, Behind DPS School,
Near Malvan Mandir, Dumas Road,
Surat, Gujarat - 395007

🗺️ Google Maps: https://goo.gl/maps/tbJVinE8joDNvqbZ6

The campus is situated on the banks of the Tapi River and is spread across 100 acres of land. The nearest BRTS station is ONGC Nagar. City Bus No. 206 runs between Chowk Station and the college campus every 30 minutes.

knowledge_base.question: What is the college timing / working hours?
knowledge_base.answer:
CKPCET office timings are 9:30 AM to 5:10 PM, Monday to Saturday.

Academic classes typically run from 8:30 AM to 4:30 PM. The college follows the GTU academic calendar. Students are expected to maintain a minimum 75% attendance as required by GTU norms.

knowledge_base.question: What courses / programs are offered at CKPCET?
knowledge_base.answer:
CKPCET offers the following programs:

🎓 Undergraduate (B.E.) — 4-year programs:
• Computer Engineering
• Information Technology
• Mechanical Engineering
• Civil Engineering
• Electrical Engineering
• Electronics & Communication Engineering

🎓 Postgraduate (M.E.) programs are also offered in select disciplines.

All B.E. programs are 4-year courses divided into 8 semesters, following the GTU curriculum. All programs are AICTE approved and GTU affiliated. Degrees are awarded by Gujarat Technological University, Ahmedabad.

knowledge_base.question: What is the fee structure at CKPCET?
knowledge_base.answer:
💰 Approximate Fee Structure:

• Tuition Fee: Approximately ₹44,000 per semester for B.E. programs
• Total fees include: tuition fee, examination fee, and other institutional charges

⚠️ IMPORTANT: Fees are subject to change every academic year as per the Fee Regulatory Committee (FRC) guidelines set by the Gujarat government. The fees mentioned above are approximate and may vary. Always confirm the latest fee structure from the college admission office or the official website: https://ckpcet.ac.in

💳 Fee Payment Options:
• Online through college payment portal: https://grayquest.com/institute/ck-pithawala
• Payment modes: Debit/Credit Card, Net Banking, UPI, Bank Transfer

Fee payment deadlines are strictly followed each semester. Late payment may attract a fine.

knowledge_base.question: What are the scholarships available?
knowledge_base.answer:
CKPCET supports students in availing the following scholarship opportunities:

🏛️ Government Scholarships:
• SC/ST Scholarship — for Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe students
• SEBC Scholarship — for Socially and Educationally Backward Classes
• EWS Scholarship — for Economically Weaker Sections
• Minority/OBC scholarships as per state guidelines

🏆 Merit-Based Aid:
• Top-ranking students in GTU examinations may receive merit recognition
• Some departmental scholarships are available

Students must apply for government scholarships through the official state scholarship portal after admission. The college admission office and welfare cell actively assist students with scholarship applications, documentation, and follow-up.

knowledge_base.question: Can I get an education loan?
knowledge_base.answer:
Yes, CKPCET provides education loan assistance to students. The college:

🏦 Education Loan Support:
• Maintains tie-ups with partner banks for student education loans
• Provides necessary bonafide certificates and recommendation letters for loan applications
• Guides students through the loan process

Contact the admission or student welfare office during working hours (+91 63550 55839) for help with loan applications and to get the list of partner banks. Most nationalized banks like SBI, Bank of Baroda, etc., offer education loans for accredited engineering colleges.

knowledge_base.question: What is the refund policy for fees?
knowledge_base.answer:
CKPCET's fee refund policy follows GTU and AICTE guidelines:

• If a student withdraws admission before the academic session starts — higher refund possible
• If withdrawal is after the session starts — refund depends on timing and is governed by AICTE norms

For the exact refund schedule please contact the college administration office:
📞 +91 63550 55839
📧 contact@ckpcet.ac.in

Note: Fees paid to ACPC/GTU are subject to their own refund policy.

knowledge_base.question: What is the admission process at CKPCET?
knowledge_base.answer:
Admissions at CKPCET are conducted through ACPC (Admission Committee for Professional Courses), Government of Gujarat.

📋 Step-by-Step Admission Process:
1. ✅ Pass 12th Science with Physics and Mathematics as compulsory subjects
2. ✅ Appear for GUJCET (Gujarat Common Entrance Test) or JEE Main
3. ✅ Register on the ACPC portal: https://acpc.gujarat.gov.in/
4. ✅ Fill your branch choices during ACPC online counseling rounds
5. ✅ Receive seat allotment letter (if allotted)
6. ✅ Attend document verification at CKPCET
7. ✅ Pay admission fees to confirm your seat

ACPC typically conducts 3-4 counseling rounds from July to August after 12th results. Management quota admissions are also available — contact the admission office directly for details.

knowledge_base.question: What is GUJCET and how do I register?
knowledge_base.answer:
GUJCET (Gujarat Common Entrance Test) is the state-level entrance exam for engineering admissions in Gujarat.

📝 Key Details:
• Subject Coverage: Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics
• It is conducted by the Gujarat Secondary and Higher Secondary Education Board (GSEB)
• Registration typically opens in March–April each year
• Results are used by ACPC for seat allotment

For current GUJCET dates, registration, and notification:
🔗 ACPC Official Portal: https://acpc.gujarat.gov.in/

JEE Main score is also accepted for admissions under the ACPC process.

knowledge_base.question: What is the eligibility criteria for admission?
knowledge_base.answer:
To be eligible for B.E. admission at CKPCET:

📚 Academic Requirements:
• Must have passed 12th Science (HSC) with Physics and Mathematics as compulsory subjects
• Minimum aggregate marks: 45% for Open Category students
• Minimum aggregate marks: 40% for Reserved Categories (SC/ST/OBC/SEBC)

🔖 Entrance Exam:
• Must have appeared in GUJCET or JEE Main

📇 Other Requirements:
• Must be an Indian national
• Age: As per AICTE norms (no upper age limit currently)
• Domicile requirements as per ACPC state-level admission rules

For management quota, contact the college directly.

knowledge_base.question: What documents are required for admission?
knowledge_base.answer:
The following documents are required at the time of document verification for admission at CKPCET:

📁 Required Documents:
✅ 10th Mark Sheet (all semester marksheets)
✅ 12th Mark Sheet (all semester marksheets)
✅ GUJCET / JEE Main Scorecard
✅ School Leaving Certificate (LC)
✅ Transfer Certificate (TC) — if applicable
✅ Caste Certificate — SC/ST/SEBC/EWS (if applicable)
✅ Non-Creamy Layer Certificate — for OBC/SEBC (if applicable)
✅ Income Certificate (family income)
✅ Domicile Certificate (Gujarat domicile)
✅ Aadhaar Card
✅ Passport-size photographs (recent, 4–6 copies)
✅ Migration Certificate — if from outside Gujarat Board
✅ ACPC Seat Allotment Letter
✅ Medical fitness certificate

⚠️ Carry originals + photocopies of all documents.

knowledge_base.question: What are the cutoff ranks for CKPCET?
knowledge_base.answer:
Cutoff ranks for CKPCET vary every year depending on:
• Number of applicants
• GUJCET/JEE Main difficulty level
• Seat availability per branch
• Reservation categories

ACPC declares cutoffs after each counseling round. Here's a general trend (approximate):

💻 Computer Engineering — Generally higher demand, closing rank varies
🖥️ Information Technology — Similar to Computer Engineering
⚙️ Mechanical Engineering — Moderate cutoff
🏗️ Civil Engineering — Generally more accessible
⚡ Electrical Engineering — Moderate
📡 Electronics & Communication — Moderate

For the most accurate and latest cutoff data:
🔗 ACPC Portal: https://acpc.gujarat.gov.in/
Check previous year merit lists for reference before applying.

knowledge_base.question: When does admission start?
knowledge_base.answer:
CKPCET admissions typically follow the ACPC academic calendar:

📅 Timeline:
• June–July: 12th results declared → ACPC registration opens
• July–August: ACPC counseling rounds (3–4 rounds)
• August–September: Document verification + fee payment at CKPCET
• September–October: Academic sessions begin

⚠️ Note: Dates vary every year. Always check:
🔗 ACPC Portal: https://acpc.gujarat.gov.in/
🔗 CKPCET Website: https://ckpcet.ac.in

Management quota admissions (with different timelines) are also available. Contact +91 78628-24298 for management quota queries.

knowledge_base.question: Is there a management quota at CKPCET?
knowledge_base.answer:
Yes, CKPCET offers limited management quota seats as per AICTE and GTU norms.

📌 Key Details:
• Management quota seats are separate from ACPC merit-based seats
• These have a different (higher) fee structure
• Direct admission is possible without going through ACPC counseling
• Available in all branches

For management quota admissions, eligibility, and fee structure:
📞 Call: +91 78628-24298 (Admissions - BE/ME)
📞 Call: +91 63550 55839
📧 Email: contact@ckpcet.ac.in

Seat availability is limited, so contact the college early during the admission season.

knowledge_base.question: Can I change my branch after admission?
knowledge_base.answer:
Branch change (internal transfer) may be possible after the first year at CKPCET, subject to the following conditions:

• GTU rules and regulations for branch transfer must be satisfied
• Academic performance (CGPA/percentage) in first year must meet the threshold
• Seat availability in the desired branch at the time of transfer request
• College academic committee approval

Branch changes are NOT guaranteed and are decided case-by-case. Contact the Academic Section at CKPCET for detailed guidelines:
📞 +91 63550 55839
📧 contact@ckpcet.ac.in

knowledge_base.question: What is the placement record at CKPCET?
knowledge_base.answer:
CKPCET has a strong placement record with consistent results over the years:

📊 Placement Highlights:
• Overall Placement Rate: 85%+ of eligible students
• Average Package: ₹4.5 LPA (Lakhs Per Annum)
• Highest Package: ₹12 LPA (varies yearly)

🏆 Top Performers:

• Final year students with 60%+ and no active backlogs are eligible

📅 Placement Process:
• Pre-placement training is conducted by the T&P Cell
• Training includes: aptitude tests, technical interviews, soft skills, resume building, and mock interviews
• Campus drives are organized from August–March every year

For more info, contact the Training and Placement Cell during office hours.

knowledge_base.question: How does the placement process work?
knowledge_base.answer:
The Training & Placement (T&P) Cell at CKPCET manages the entire placement process:

📋 Student Eligibility:
• Minimum 60% aggregate marks (no active backlogs)
• Must be a registered final-year student

📚 Pre-Placement Training (conducted throughout the year):
1. Aptitude / Quantitative Reasoning
2. Verbal and Communication Skills
3. Technical Interview Preparation (DSA, OS, DBMS, etc.)
4. Resume Building and LinkedIn Profile
5. Group Discussions and Mock Interviews
6. Company-specific preparation sessions

🏢 On-Campus Drives:
• Companies visit campus for written tests, group discussions, and interviews
• Results are declared quickly; selected students receive offer letters

For placement queries, contact the Training and Placement Department during college hours.

knowledge_base.question: Are internships available for students?
knowledge_base.answer:
Yes! CKPCET actively facilitates internships for students:

🏢 Internship Types:

• Research internships through faculty contacts

👨‍💼 How to Apply:
• Register with the T&P Cell during the desired semester
• T&P Cell floats internship notifications throughout the year
• Students can also apply for SSIP (Student Startup and Innovation Policy) internship grants from the Gujarat government

Contact the T&P Cell for the latest internship openings.

knowledge_base.question: Is there a hostel facility at CKPCET?

• Attached toilet and bathroom
• Geysers for hot water
• Study table and chairs in each room
• 24/7 RO purified drinking water

🍽️ Other Facilities:
• Mess with vegetarian food (breakfast, lunch, dinner)
• Sports facilities: Indoor games, outdoor games, multipurpose ground
• Lush green surroundings

💵 Hostel Fees:
For the latest hostel fee structure, visit: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1TS9RdhD-jUShRojsu9ji19P9gSwQk9dH/view?usp=sharing

📋 Hostel Registration Form: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1axFKG_F9U0SqD-Nne36DuOvi-lqTx7Ww/view?usp=sharing

knowledge_base.question: What hostel rules are there?
knowledge_base.answer:
CKPCET hostel operates with strict discipline rules to ensure a safe and focused environment:

⚠️ Key Rules Include:

• Ragging is strictly prohibited and punishable

• Cleanliness of rooms and common areas is mandatory

📋 For the complete and current list of hostel rules:

knowledge_base.question: Is CKPCET accredited?
knowledge_base.answer:
Yes, CKPCET has accreditations from prestigious bodies:

🏆 NBA Accreditation:
• Computer Engineering — Accredited by the National Board of Accreditation (NBA), New Delhi
• Civil Engineering — Accredited by NBA, New Delhi

📋 Approvals and Affiliations:
• Affiliated with Gujarat Technological University (GTU), Ahmedabad

🔗 Mandatory Disclosure: https://ckpcet.ac.in/docs/about-us/man-dis.pdf

knowledge_base.question: What is GTU?
knowledge_base.answer:
GTU stands for Gujarat Technological University, headquartered in Ahmedabad, Gujarat.

📋 GTU Key Facts:
• Government university established in 2007 by the Government of Gujarat
• Affiliates 494+ self-financed engineering, pharmacy, and management colleges across Gujarat
• CKPCET is affiliated with GTU
• GTU prescribes the curriculum, time tables, and examination pattern for all affiliated colleges
• Degrees (B.E., M.E.) are awarded by GTU

🔗 GTU Official Website: https://www.gtu.ac.in

All exam results, hall tickets, and academic calendars are published on the GTU portal.

knowledge_base.question: What is ACPC?
knowledge_base.answer:
ACPC stands for Admission Committee for Professional Courses, Gujarat.

📋 ACPC Key Facts:
• Government body that manages centralized admissions to all engineering, pharmacy, and MBA colleges in Gujarat
• Conducts merit-based counseling using GUJCET and JEE Main scores
• Students must register on ACPC portal for engineering college admissions
• ACPC allots seats and manages all counseling rounds

🔗 ACPC Official Portal: https://acpc.gujarat.gov.in/
🔗 JACPC (old URL): http://www.jacpcldce.ac.in/

Always check the ACPC portal for the latest registration dates and counseling schedule.

knowledge_base.question: What is the college affiliation?
knowledge_base.answer:
C. K. Pithawala College of Engineering and Technology (CKPCET) is:

🏛️ Affiliated with: Gujarat Technological University (GTU), Ahmedabad
📋 Managed by: Navyug Vidyabhavan Trust
✅ Approved by: AICTE (All India Council for Technical Education)
🏆 Accredited by: NBA (for Computer Engineering and Civil Engineering departments)

All B.E. and M.E. degrees are awarded by GTU. The institute has been operational since December 1998. The Trust also manages several other educational institutions in Surat including Navyug Arts, Science, and Commerce Colleges.

knowledge_base.question: What is the alumni network like?
knowledge_base.answer:
CKPCET has a strong and growing alumni network:

👥 Alumni Highlights:
• Alumni are placed at leading MNCs and regional companies across India and abroad

knowledge_base.question: What is the exam and result process at CKPCET?
knowledge_base.answer:
CKPCET follows the GTU examination pattern:

📝 Examination Pattern:
• Theory exams: 70 marks (end semester, conducted by GTU)
• Internal assessment: 30 marks (CIE — Continuous Internal Evaluation)
• Practical exams: As per GTU curriculum

📋 Result Declaration:
• Results are published on the GTU portal: https://www.gtu.ac.in
• Students get their mark sheets from the college after GTU declares results
• Any RTI or result-related queries must be directed to GTU

⚠️ Backlog/ATKT Policy:
• Students can appear for backlog exams in subsequent semesters
• ATKT (Allowed to Keep Terms) rules are as per GTU regulations
• Maximum number of attempts per subject follows GTU policy

knowledge_base.question: What are the course curriculum and time tables?
knowledge_base.answer:
The course curriculum at CKPCET follows the GTU-prescribed syllabus for each branch:

📚 Curriculum Details:
• Syllabus is set by Gujarat Technological University (GTU)
• Regular updates align the curriculum with industry needs
• Mix of theory, practical, and project work
• Final year project is a mandatory component

🕐 Time Tables:
• Semester-wise time tables are published on the CKPCET website:
🔗 https://ckpcet.ac.in/about/academics/time-tables

📖 Course Curriculum:
🔗 https://ckpcet.ac.in/about/academics/curriculum

For branch-specific syllabi, visit the GTU website: https://www.gtu.ac.in

knowledge_base.question: Who manages CKPCET?
knowledge_base.answer:
CKPCET is managed by the Navyug Vidyabhavan Trust:

🏛️ Trust Details:
• Navyug Vidyabhavan Trust, Surat
• Registered under Bombay Public Trust Act 1950 on 21 February 1965
• A Public Education Trust committed to quality education

🎓 Other Institutions Under the Trust:
• Navyug Arts College
• Navyug Science College
• Navyug Commerce College
• Maniben Pithawalla Industrial Training Institute
• C. K. Pithawalla Institute of Pharmaceutical Science & Research
• C. K. Pithawala College of Engineering & Technology

The trust has a strong legacy of over 50 years in education.

knowledge_base.answer:
The official CKPCET website is:
🌐 https://ckpcet.ac.in

For specific pages:

knowledge_base.question: What is the Computer Engineering department like?
knowledge_base.answer:
The Computer Engineering (CE) department is one of the most popular and well-equipped departments at CKPCET:

💻 Department Highlights:
• NBA Accredited — recognized for quality education
• Strong placement record with multiple offers per student
• Modern computer labs with high-end systems
• Focus on: DSA, OS, DBMS, Web Development, Cloud, AI/ML
• Active Coding Club and hackathon participation

🎓 Program: B.E. in Computer Engineering (4 years, 8 semesters, GTU affiliated)

🏢 Top Recruiters for CE students:
TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Cognizant, HCL, Tech Mahindra, and many Surat-based IT firms

For department-specific queries, contact the HOD – Computer Engineering at the college.

knowledge_base.question: What is the Information Technology department like?
knowledge_base.answer:
The Information Technology (IT) department at CKPCET offers a strong program:

🖥️ Department Highlights:
• Focus on networking, web development, cybersecurity, software engineering
• Modern labs and industry-aligned curriculum
• Strong placement outcomes
• Active participation in hackathons and coding events

🎓 Program: B.E. in Information Technology (4 years, 8 semesters, GTU affiliated)

For IT department queries, contact the Department Office during college hours.

knowledge_base.question: What is the Mechanical Engineering department like?
knowledge_base.answer:
The Mechanical Engineering (ME) department at CKPCET:

⚙️ Department Highlights:
• Comprehensive labs: Thermodynamics, Fluid Mechanics, Manufacturing, CAD/CAM
• Workshop facility with fitting, welding, and machining equipment
• Industry visits to local and regional manufacturing companies
• Participation in BAJA SAE and other mechanical competitions

🎓 Program: B.E. in Mechanical Engineering (4 years, 8 semesters, GTU affiliated)

🏢 Typical Recruiters: L&T, GSFC, ONGC, and engineering firms in South Gujarat

Contact the HOD – Mechanical Engineering for department-specific information.

knowledge_base.question: What is the Civil Engineering department like?
knowledge_base.answer:
The Civil Engineering department at CKPCET:

🏗️ Department Highlights:
• NBA Accredited — one of the two accredited departments
• Modern labs: Materials Testing, Structural Engineering, Geotechnical, Surveying
• Strong focus on construction, design, and environmental engineering
• Industry tie-ups with construction companies in Surat

🎓 Program: B.E. in Civil Engineering (4 years, 8 semesters, GTU affiliated)

Surat's rapid urban development offers great internship and career opportunities for civil engineering graduates from CKPCET.

Contact the HOD – Civil Engineering for department-specific information.

knowledge_base.question: What is the Electrical Engineering department like?
knowledge_base.answer:
The Electrical Engineering (EE) department at CKPCET:

⚡ Department Highlights:
• Labs: Electrical Machines, Power Electronics, Power Systems
• Industry-aligned curriculum covering power generation, transmission, and control
• Students work with motors, generators, and electronic drives
• Internship opportunities with GSECL, DGVCL, and private electrical firms

🎓 Program: B.E. in Electrical Engineering (4 years, 8 semesters, GTU affiliated)

Contact the HOD – Electrical Engineering for department-specific information.

knowledge_base.question: What is the Electronics and Communication Engineering department like?
knowledge_base.answer:
The Electronics & Communication Engineering (EC) department at CKPCET:

📡 Department Highlights:
• Labs: Electronics & Circuits, Communication Systems, Microprocessor, VLSI
• Strong focus on IoT, embedded systems, signal processing, and wireless communication
• Active Electronics Club with project competitions
• Growing demand for EC engineers in IoT and telecom sectors

🎓 Program: B.E. in Electronics & Communication Engineering (4 years, 8 semesters, GTU affiliated)

Contact the HOD – Electronics & Communication for department-specific information.

knowledge_base.question: Does CKPCET have a gym or fitness center?
knowledge_base.answer:
CKPCET does not currently have a dedicated gym/fitness center on campus. However, students can enjoy:

⚽ Sports Facilities:
• Cricket ground
• Football / Hockey ground
• Badminton and volleyball courts
• Table tennis and indoor games
• Athletic track

Students are encouraged to participate in sports as part of their overall development. Annual sports events are organized by the college.

knowledge_base.question: What is NCC / NSS at CKPCET?
knowledge_base.answer:
CKPCET offers participation in extracurricular programs like NCC (National Cadet Corps) and NSS (National Service Scheme):

🎖️ NCC (National Cadet Corps):
• Voluntary military training program
• Students receive training in discipline, leadership, and national service
• NCC participation can award extra marks in some government jobs

🌱 NSS (National Service Scheme):
• Community service-oriented program
• Students participate in social activities, awareness camps, blood donation drives, and rural development initiatives

For enrollment in NCC/NSS, contact the respective program officer through the college office.

knowledge_base.question: Can I visit the CKPCET campus?
knowledge_base.answer:
Yes! We welcome prospective students and parents for a campus visit:

🏫 Campus Visit Details:
• Campus visits are possible during working hours (9:30 AM – 5:10 PM, Monday–Saturday)
• Contact the Admission Office to schedule your visit in advance
• You can tour the labs, library, hostels, and classrooms

📞 Contact to Schedule Visit:
• Phone: +91 63550 55839 or +91 63550 62275
• Email: contact@ckpcet.ac.in
• Walk-in visits are also generally welcome

🗺️ Campus Address:
Opposite Surat Airport, Behind DPS School, Near Malvan Mandir, Dumas Road, Surat - 395007

🗺️ Google Maps: https://goo.gl/maps/tbJVinE8joDNvqbZ6

knowledge_base.question: What is CKPCET's NIRF ranking?
knowledge_base.answer:
CKPCET participates in the NIRF (National Institutional Ranking Framework) by the Ministry of Education, India.

📊 For the most current NIRF ranking data:
• Visit: https://www.nirfindia.org/Home and search for CKPCET
• Or visit the college's mandatory disclosure: https://ckpcet.ac.in/docs/about-us/man-dis.pdf

🏆 The college is listed on NIRF and recognized by UGC, AICTE, and GTU.

For the exact ranking and performance index, check the NIRF portal for the latest year's data.

knowledge_base.question: What is the SSIP program?
knowledge_base.answer:
SSIP stands for Student Startup and Innovation Policy — a Gujarat government initiative:

💡 SSIP at CKPCET:
• Students with innovative project ideas can get funding of up to ₹2 lakh per team
• The program encourages student entrepreneurship and product development
• CKPCET has a dedicated SSIP cell to guide and support student innovators
• Projects in areas like IoT, agriculture, health tech, green energy, etc., qualify

📋 How to Apply:
• Contact the SSIP Coordinator at CKPCET
• Present your idea to the department SSIP committee
• Successful ideas get mentorship and seed funding from the Gujarat government

This is a great opportunity for students to build real-world projects while still in college!

knowledge_base.question: What is the attendance requirement at CKPCET?
knowledge_base.answer:
CKPCET follows GTU's attendance regulations:

📋 Attendance Policy:
• Minimum 75% attendance is mandatory in each subject
• Students with less than 75% attendance may not be allowed to appear in the end-semester GTU exam (marked as Detained - DT)
• Students should track attendance through the official student portal

⚠️ Medical Leave:
• Medical certificates can be submitted for genuine illness-related absences
• The department heads review such cases individually

It is strongly advised to maintain good attendance to avoid being barred from exams and to maintain a good academic record.

knowledge_base.question: How do I pay college fees online?
knowledge_base.answer:
You can pay CKPCET fees online through the following portal:

💻 Online Fee Payment:
🔗 https://grayquest.com/institute/ck-pithawala

💳 Available Payment Modes:
• Debit Card / Credit Card
• Net Banking
• UPI (Google Pay, PhonePe, BHIM, etc.)
• Bank Transfer (NEFT/RTGS)

⚠️ Important:
• Fee payment deadlines are strictly enforced each semester
• Always keep the payment receipt / confirmation for your records
• For issues with online payment, contact: contact@ckpcet.ac.in or call +91 63550 55839

knowledge_base.question: What happens after ACPC seat allotment?
knowledge_base.answer:
After receiving your ACPC seat allotment letter:

✅ Step-by-Step Process:
1. Download the ACPC Allotment Letter from the ACPC portal
2. Report to CKPCET on the specified date for document verification
3. Bring all original documents + photocopies
4. Documents will be verified by the admission office
5. Pay the first semester fee (online or offline as specified)
6. Collect your enrollment number and admission confirmation
7. Attend the orientation / induction program
8. Classes begin as per the GTU academic calendar

📞 For any doubt, call the CKPCET Admission Office:
+91 78628-24298 (BE/ME admissions)
+91 63550 55839 (General)

⚠️ Do NOT miss the reporting deadline — failing to report forfeits your seat.
keywords: what to do after seat allotment

knowledge_base.question: What are reservation categories in Gujarat engineering admissions?
knowledge_base.answer:
Gujarat engineering admissions through ACPC follow state reservation policies:

📋 Category-wise Seat Reservation:
• SC (Scheduled Caste): 7%
• ST (Scheduled Tribe): 15%
• SEBC (Socially and Educationally Backward Classes): 27%
• EWS (Economically Weaker Section): 10%
• General/Open Category: Remaining seats

📌 Additional Reservations:
• Physically Disabled (PH): Horizontal reservation across categories
• NRI / Management Quota: Additional seats outside state quota

🔗 For exact details and latest reservation percentages:
https://acpc.gujarat.gov.in/

Always check your category certificate before applying to avail the correct reservation benefits.

knowledge_base.question: Is there a women's helpline at CKPCET?
knowledge_base.answer:
Yes, CKPCET has a 24x7 Women Helpline:
📞 +91 90990 63009

This is available round the clock for women students and staff. The college also has a Women Development Cell and Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) for any gender-related concerns.

knowledge_base.question: Does CKPCET have a medical center or health facility?
knowledge_base.answer:
Yes, CKPCET has an on-campus Medical Center:

🏥 Medical Facility:
• First aid and basic medical care available on campus
• Medical staff available during college hours
• Located within the campus residential zone
• Hostel students have access to medical support

For emergencies, the nearest hospitals in Surat are accessible via college transport or auto/cab from campus.

🔗 More info: https://ckpcet.ac.in/resources/direct/medical

knowledge_base.question: What is the MYSY scholarship scheme?
knowledge_base.answer:
MYSY stands for Mukhyamantri Yuva Swavalamban Yojana — a Gujarat government scholarship for meritorious students from economically weaker families:

💡 MYSY Details:
• For students scoring 80%+ in 12th Standard
• Family income must be below ₹6 lakh per annum
• Provides tuition fee support + hostel allowance
• Applicable for engineering, medical, and diploma courses

📋 Apply at: https://mysy.guj.nic.in

🔗 CKPCET MYSY Info: https://ckpcet.ac.in/activities/community/mysy

Contact the college scholarship cell for assistance with MYSY application.

knowledge_base.question: What is the CMSS scholarship?
knowledge_base.answer:
CMSS stands for Chief Minister's Scholarship Scheme for students pursuing professional courses in Gujarat:

💡 CMSS Details:
• For students from general/open category with family income below a specified limit
• Covers partial tuition fees for engineering students
• Applied through the Gujarat government scholarship portal

🔗 CMSS Application: https://drive.google.com/file/d/0B1N9snDSA9nISzloaXJKcHRzNFJKaU8yeXFDV0U1TGl0N3c4/view?usp=sharing

Contact the CKPCET admission office for guidance on applying for CMSS.

knowledge_base.question: Does CKPCET have a language or communication skill center?
knowledge_base.answer:
Yes! CKPCET has a dedicated Center of Language Proficiency and Personality Enrichment (CLP):

🗣️ CLP Features:
• Focuses on English language communication skills
• Personality development and soft skills training
• Helps students prepare for interviews and professional life
• Especially useful for students from Gujarati-medium backgrounds
• Sessions are conducted throughout the semester

🔗 More info: https://ckpcet.ac.in/resources/central/clp

This center is very helpful for placement preparation and professional communication.

knowledge_base.question: Does CKPCET have a stationery store or shop on campus?
knowledge_base.answer:
Yes, CKPCET has an on-campus Stationery Store:

🛒 Store Details:
• Located within the campus for student convenience
• Stocks stationery, drawing materials, and basic academic supplies
• Saves students the trip to off-campus shops

🔗 More info: https://ckpcet.ac.in/resources/central/store

Additionally, the campus has a bank, ATM, and convenience facilities for day-to-day student needs.

knowledge_base.question: Does CKPCET offer higher studies support or guidance?
knowledge_base.answer:
Yes, CKPCET's T&P Cell provides higher studies guidance:

🎓 Higher Studies Options:
• M.E. / M.Tech programs (postgraduate) — available at CKPCET itself
• GATE exam guidance for admission to IITs/NITs

📋 Support Provided:

knowledge_base.question: What is the SHODH scheme at CKPCET?
knowledge_base.answer:
SHODH is a Gujarat government scholarship scheme for research-oriented students:

🔬 SHODH Scheme Details:
• Full form: State-funded Higher Education scheme for Research and Development
• Provides financial support to students pursuing research and postgraduate studies
• Encourages technical innovation and academic research
• CKPCET students can apply through the scheme

🔗 CKPCET SHODH Info: https://ckpcet.ac.in/activities/community/shodh

Contact the college office for eligibility and application details.

knowledge_base.question: What is the Vishwakarma Yojana at CKPCET?
knowledge_base.answer:
PM Vishwakarma Yojana is a Government of India scheme supporting traditional artisans and craftspeople:

🔨 Vishwakarma Yojana at CKPCET:
• CKPCET participates in community outreach under this scheme
• Helps connect engineering knowledge with traditional crafts and skill upgradation
• Students and faculty participate in awareness and training programs

🔗 More info: https://ckpcet.ac.in/activities/community/vishwakarma

Contact the college office for the latest activities under this scheme.

knowledge_base.question: Is there a workshop facility at CKPCET?
knowledge_base.answer:
Yes, CKPCET has a well-equipped Central Workshop:

🔧 Workshop Facilities:
• Fitting shop, welding shop, machining shop
• Carpentry and sheet metal work
• Foundry and forging equipment
• Used by Mechanical and Civil Engineering students for practical training
• Also open for interdisciplinary project work

🔗 More info: https://ckpcet.ac.in/resources/central/workshop

The workshop is a key facility for hands-on engineering practice and is part of the mandatory GTU curriculum for relevant branches.

knowledge_base.question: What is the Central Computer Centre at CKPCET?
knowledge_base.answer:
CKPCET has a Central Computer Centre (CCC) available to all students:

💻 CCC Features:
• Large computer lab with high-end systems
• Available to students from all branches
• High-speed internet connectivity
• Used for programming, research, online courses, and project work
• Software tools: MS Office, AutoCAD, MATLAB, and more

🔗 More info: https://ckpcet.ac.in/resources/central/ccc

The CCC supplements department-specific labs and provides additional computing resources.

knowledge_base.question: Does CKPCET have MOUs with industries?
knowledge_base.answer:
Yes, CKPCET has signed MOUs (Memoranda of Understanding) with various industries and organizations:

🤝 MOU Benefits for Students:
• Industry collaboration for internships and projects
• Joint workshops and training programs
• Faculty and student exchange programs
• Access to industry expertise and resources
• Enhanced placement opportunities through partner companies

🔗 View MOUs: https://ckpcet.ac.in/about/direct/mou

These industry partnerships strengthen CKPCET's connection with the real world and improve student career prospects.

knowledge_base.question: What is the IQAC at CKPCET?
knowledge_base.answer:
IQAC stands for Internal Quality Assurance Cell:

📋 IQAC Role at CKPCET:
• Ensures continuous improvement in academic and administrative quality
• Monitors teaching-learning processes, faculty development, and student outcomes
• Prepares Annual Quality Assurance Reports (AQAR)
• Works towards maintaining accreditation standards (NBA, AICTE)
• Collects student feedback and acts on it

🔗 IQAC Page: https://ckpcet.ac.in/cells/iqac-cell

The IQAC is a key body that keeps the institute's quality standards high and ensures CKPCET continues to meet national education benchmarks.

knowledge_base.question: Can physically disabled students get support at CKPCET?
knowledge_base.answer:
Yes, CKPCET has provisions for physically disabled students:

♿ Support Available:
• Physical Disability Grievance Redressal Cell — dedicated cell for PD students
• Reservation in admissions as per ACPC/GTU horizontal reservation policy
• Campus accessibility support
• Fee concessions may apply under government schemes

📞 Contact:
• Physical Disability Grievance Cell: https://ckpcet.ac.in/cells/pdgr
• General: contact@ckpcet.ac.in
• Phone: +91 63550 55839

PD students are encouraged to contact the college during admission for specific accommodation needs.

knowledge_base.question: What are the cultural clubs at CKPCET?
knowledge_base.answer:
CKPCET has a vibrant set of cultural clubs for student creativity:

🎨 Cultural Clubs:
• Dance Club — classical, folk, and western dance
• Drama Club — stage performances and theatre

• Literature Club — creative writing, poetry, and debates

Students can join multiple clubs and participate in inter-college competitions and annual festivals.
